name: Shawn McCarthy
title: Vice President, Chief Architect, Global Architecture, Risk and Governance | PhD Candidate, December 2026
summary: Enterprise architecture and AI governance leader with 20+ years modernizing complex financial services technology portfolios. Known for turning architectural evidence into strategy, product operating models, security-left governance, and measurable executive decision systems.
atsSummary: Enterprise architecture and AI governance executive with 20+ years modernizing complex financial services technology portfolios. Leads global architecture, application security, and information risk teams across multinational environments. Known for turning architectural evidence into strategy, product operating models, security-left governance, and platform modernization.
impactHighlights: Lead a 72-person global architecture, application security, and information risk organization supporting approximately 655 applications across North America, Asia, and Europe.
Transformed fragmented architecture governance into a federated, outcome-driven capability embedded in business strategy, risk management, and delivery execution.
Recognized as a Forrester 2025 Enterprise Architecture Awards finalist for Project Atlas, an architect development and practice maturity program.
Evolved portfolio planning from disposition-based application roadmaps to strategic business blueprints across Global Retail, Global Retirement, and Institutional businesses.
Led a 200-person engineering organization at Vantiv across API products, developer portals, prepaid platforms, secure delivery, and PCI/SOX control environments.

roles.title: Vice President, Chief Architect, Global Architecture, Risk and Governance
bullets: Lead enterprise architecture, application security, and information risk capabilities for a global financial services enterprise.
Manage architecture strategy for a multinational portfolio spanning insurance, wealth, retirement, and institutional business lines.
Built a federated architecture operating model connecting business capability, application portfolio, security risk, governance evidence, and delivery execution.
Guide modernization strategy across legacy systems, cloud platforms, APIs, AI delivery, and security-left governance.

roles.title: Managing Director, Platform and Application Architecture
bullets: Led centralized solution architecture for digital experience, trading, mobile, digital messaging, CI/CD, APIs, cloud, and wealth management platforms.
Developed technology strategy blueprints and multi-year roadmaps for a 1,200+ application portfolio.
Established architecture practices for platform strategy, 4+1 architecture views, modernization, and technical debt management.
Created the N.E.R.D. talent development program to accelerate cross-functional engineering and architecture capability.

roles.title: Head of Portals and APIs / Director, Application Development
bullets: Led a 200-person engineering organization across API solutions, developer portals, prepaid platform delivery, and secure application development.
Advanced API-as-a-product, developer experience, SOA, ESB, and modern .NET architecture patterns for transaction-processing platforms.
Directed platform improvements across automation, test automation, monitoring, logging, security frameworks, encryption, data warehousing, reporting, and responsive web strategy.
Served across application security, agile change, PCI/SOX audit, intern development, and organizational transformation initiatives.

roles.title: Graduate Instructor and Advisory Board Member
bullets: Teach graduate-level technology courses covering generative AI, LLM applications, cloud deployment, DevSecOps, APIs, and test automation.
State-approved continuing education provider for Generative AI Deep Learning Ethics for licensed insurance professionals.

credentials: PhD Candidate, Computer Science and Information Systems, expected December 2026
University of Colorado Denver, Graduate Instructor
Platinum NVIDIA Deep Learning Institute Instructor
Certified across the NVIDIA generative deep learning suite
publications: From Gatekeepers to Launchpads: How Autonomous Governance Transforms Enterprise Architecture - Metis / IASA Global, 2026
The Engineering Imperative: Why AI Will Not Replace Your Best Developers - CIO.com, 2025
From Fixed Frameworks to Strategic Enablers: Architecting AI Transformation - CIO.com, 2025
Cross-Document Emotion Consistency Framework for Financial Disclosure Risk Screening - Journal of Risk and Financial Management, 2026

web.title: Vice President, Chief Architect, Global Architecture, Risk and Governance

summary.lead: Distinguished enterprise architecture executive and PhD Candidate
summary.text: with 25+ years transforming complex global organizations through innovative technology strategies. Currently serving as Vice President at Manulife, managing a comprehensive architecture practice that has evolved from a fragmented, ad-hoc function into a federated, outcome-driven capability deeply embedded in business strategy and delivery. Recognized thought leader with major publications in InfoWorld and CIO.com, passionate about advancing AI technologies and enterprise transformation while completing a PhD in Computer Science and Information Systems, expected December 2026.

publications.title: Cross-Document Emotion Consistency (CDEC): A Feature Family Framework for Financial Disclosure Risk Screening
publications.details: Journal of Risk and Financial Management (JRFM) | 2026
publications.description: Framework measuring emotional alignment between SEC filings and earnings call Q&A using 28-dimensional emotion classification, achieving twice the risk separation of standard sentiment baselines for financial disclosure screening.

publications.title: A Momentum-Based Normalization Framework for Generating Profitable Analyst Sentiment Signals
publications.description: Momentum-based normalization framework for standardizing diverse analyst recommendations into profitable investment signals using firm-relative ECDF and expanding global quantile classification.

publications.title: From Gatekeepers to Launchpads: How Autonomous Governance Transforms Enterprise Architecture
publications.description: Argues that the decision rate of AI-driven development has outrun any process that inspects decisions individually. Frames Architecture as Code, Risk as Code, Operations as Code, and Security as Code as the four-pillar convergence that turns architectural governance into a launchpad rather than a gate.

publications.title: The Engineering Imperative: Why AI Won't Replace Your Best Developers
publications.description: Examines why AI will elevate rather than replace skilled engineers, introducing the 70/30 rule for AI-generated code and exploring the evolution from reactive maintenance to strategic architectural design.

publications.title: From Fixed Frameworks to Strategic Enablers: Architecting AI Transformation
publications.description: Explores how traditional architectural approaches have become unsustainable for AI-driven enterprises. Argues for strategic enablement throughout the AI transformation lifecycle.

publications.title: Security is dead: Long live risk management
publications.description: A CIO's guide to reimagining risk management in modern software development. Explores transformation from reactive security gatekeeping to proactive risk enablement.

publications.title: How to leverage APIs for IT-enabled information capability
publications.description: Explores how APIs can transform information architecture into strategic business capability through real-time data access and integration.

publications.title: From project to product: Architecting the future of enterprise technology
publications.description: Discusses transitioning from project-based thinking to product-centric architecture for continuous innovation and sustainable growth.

publications.title: Fin-ALICE: Artificial Linguistic Intelligence Causal Econometrics
publications.details: Journal of Risk and Financial Management (JRFM) | 2024
publications.description: Novel framework for financial forecasting integrating emotional sentiment analysis, supply chain dynamics, and advanced machine learning techniques.

publications.title: The Emotion Magnitude Effect: Navigating Market Dynamics Amidst Supply Chain Events
publications.details: Journal of Risk and Financial Management (JRFM) | 2023
publications.description: Presents novel emotion magnitude metric capturing emotional undercurrents in financial markets during 2019-2021 using knowledge graphs and advanced NLP techniques.

publications.title: Enhancing Financial Market Analysis with Emotion Corpora and News Co-Occurrence Networks
publications.details: Journal of Risk and Financial Management (JRFM) | 2022
publications.description: Applied NLP to analyze 500,000+ financial news articles, identifying economic activity patterns and inter-company relationships using emotion corpora and advanced AI techniques.

publications.title: Patent: System And Methods For Transaction-based Process Management
publications.description: Innovative system design for automated transaction processing and workflow management in enterprise environments.
